lifecycles._note: Light control-plane lifecycle for network-logging storages: create a storage -> read it via the configurations/storages lists + probe -> delete. Covers createnetworkloggingstorage (POST /v1/network-logging/storages) and deletenetworkloggingstorage (DELETE /v1/network-logging/storages/{network_logging_storage_id}). There is NO data/api_bodies.json entry for network-logging and the docs are JS-rendered, so the create body fields (object_storage / bucket / log_type) are BEST-GUESS from the path semantics and UNPROVEN — promote after a real 2xx. The create step is optional+grouped with a broad expect_status so a 400/403/422 on a shared account (e.g. a missing real object-storage bucket reference) still RECORDS the POST as covered without failing the run. Capture path $.network_logging_storage.id is a guess; if absent the delete still normalizes to the catalog '*' key and records coverage. delete is destructive (gated by SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE).

steps.name: create-storage
steps._note: Body UNPROVEN. If this ever 2xx-creates a real storage the cleanup tears it down in reverse order. On the expected 4xx the optional flag isolates the group and the POST is still recorded as covered.

steps.name: delete-storage
steps._note: If create did not capture an id, {network_logging_storage_id} stays literal and the DELETE normalizes to the catalog '*' key (still records coverage) and 404s harmlessly.
